Scenario.title: Operation Ghost: Multi-Stage Cyber Attack on Healthcare Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex [A sophisticated multi-stage attack leveraging AI-driven reconnaissance, custom malware, and lateral movement to compromise healthcare systems and disrupt operations]
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: OS Process, Windows Registry, Application Log, Command, Network Traffic, File, Process, Domain Name Registrar, Internet-facing assets, Script
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Code Injection, Windows Registry Key Creation, Application Log Content, Process Hollowing, Pass the Hash, Command Execution, Network Traffic Flow, File Access, Process Creation, Delivery Method, Script Execution, TCP Options Probe, TCP (ISN) Sequence Predictability, IP ID Sequencing

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers deploy AI algorithms to analyze public data sources, identifying healthcare providers' IT infrastructure configurations and employee contact details, enabling precise targeting of high-value assets.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Malware is developed using automated collection scripts tailored to exploit vulnerabilities in medical devices and Picture Archiving Communication Systems (PACS), ensuring stealth and compatibility with healthcare-specific protocols.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Voice phishing calls mimic legitimate supplier communications, tricking staff into divulging credentials or initiating malicious workflows that grant initial access to internal networks.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Malicious scripts are injected into legitimate processes to execute payloads without user interaction, exploiting weak input validation in healthcare software applications.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A persistent scheduled task is created in the Windows Registry to maintain access across system reboots, ensuring long-term control over compromised endpoints.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Attackers manipulate application log content to escalate privileges, leveraging misconfigured logging mechanisms to gain administrative rights over critical systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-671: Requirements for ASIC Functionality Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Legitimate processes are hijacked using process hollowing techniques to evade endpoint detection solutions while executing malicious code.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Credentials extracted during privilege escalation are used to authenticate to other systems, expanding the attack surface across the healthcare network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network connection enumeration commands are executed to identify vulnerable hosts, shared resources, and potential pathways for lateral movement.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Remote services are exploited to move laterally between segmented networks, avoiding detection by traditional firewall rules.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Sensitive patient data, including radiology records, is exfiltrated by accessing local files on compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is transferred covertly using IDE tunneling over encrypted channels, masking exfiltration traffic as normal network activity.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Malicious deployment tools are used to encrypt electronic health records (EHRs), crippling operational capabilities and demanding ransom payments.

Scenario.summary: [Operation Ghost executes a complex, multi-phase attack on healthcare systems, combining AI-driven reconnaissance, custom malware, and advanced evasion techniques to infiltrate networks, exfiltrate sensitive data, and disrupt critical operations through ransomware deployment.]
